mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path reduces the equity discount rate and supports multiple expansion, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is in a sustained downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not above baseline after FEDFUNDS declines for two consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered instances show negative average forward return over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration forces higher expected policy rates and raises discount rates, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days after inflation prints above trend.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above its 6-month moving average by at least 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-triggered episodes do not produce lower 20-day forward returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the sign of the effect is positive in a majority of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and slower demand, so equity prices underperform over the next 60 trading days when unemployment is climbing faster than normal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes fail to show negative 60-day forward returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A broad upswing in RSAFS confirms stronger final demand and revenue growth, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not correspond to higher 20-day forward returns versus flat or negative momentum, or if the rank relationship turns negative in any sustained 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO with stable labor input indicates improving industrial supply and cyclical momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through faster earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while PAYEMS does not decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-up/PAYEMS-stable episodes do not outperform other short-horizon windows, or if the sign is non-positive in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals household demand and credit-sensitive activity, which leads S&P 500 gains over the next 60 trading days as cyclical sectors and earnings expectations strengthen.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 6-month moving average for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if housing-strength triggers fail to produce positive 60-day returns, or if the signal disappears when FEDFUNDS is already rising sharply.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, labor income growth and consumer demand improve simultaneously, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll gains and unemployment declines do not outperform baseline returns, or if triggered cases show no positive edge across any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL creates valuation pressure and tighter financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation windows do not produce negative 5-day returns, or if the sign flips positive during tightening regimes for more than half of rolling quarterly evaluations.
